title: taraxacum inundatum
intro: Taraxacum inundatum, also known as the water dandelion, is a perennial plant species within the dandelion family. The plant's leaves and stems are used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, boasting anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The leaves are rich in vitamins A, B, and C, as well as minerals like potassium and iron, offering benefits for digestive health, reducing inflammation, and boosting the immune system. Taraxacum inundatum can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and its leaves are also used in herbal capsules and tinctures for its medicinal properties.
